title: podman-docker: Support for /var/run/docker.sock
body:
### Feature request description

Hi,

I'd like to use some specific Docker accessories, including Snyk Container, to scan my images. However, they depend on `/var/run/docker.sock`, a device-y file that stock Docker provides but the podman-docker shim currently lacks.

### Suggest potential solution

Implement `/var/run/docker.sock` largely indistinguishable from Docker's `/var/run/docker.sock`.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Using ordinary Docker.

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body:
You don't specify what environment you are using as this is mainly a packaging question, the upstream repo already has a systemd tmpfile config that should create the required /run/docker.sock -> /run/podman/podman.sock symlinks

see contrib/systemd/system/podman-docker.conf

That should already be shipped as part of the podman-socker package on fedora and RHEL at least.
